passage: Physical materials can be classified by how much light passes through them. Clear glass, which is classified as transparent, allows all (or almost all) light to pass _______ wax paper, which is classified as translucent, allows only some light to pass through.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
explanation: The sentence joins two independent clauses ("Clear glass...allows all...light to pass through" and "wax paper...allows only some light to pass through"), which a semicolon can correctly connect. A comma alone creates a splice, no punctuation creates a run-on, and "and" produces the illogical reading that glass lets light pass through wax paper.
